topic: Southern Distance Quadratic — post-queue_146 patch verification (3 c5 patches)
change_summary: Queue_146 pushed 2026-04-16T03:43 citing 3 patches from c5 review: (1) Scale comparison paragraph parameter list rewritten to enumerate 4 free params with values (fixing ellipse-axis-ratio self-contradiction), (2) finding updated to sub-millimetre algorithmic precision, (3) tldr_evidence aligned to sub-millimetre. detail_evidence 5400→5402 (+2 chars), finding 68→87 (+19, now includes sub-millimetre), tldr_evidence 456→473 (+17, now sub-millimetre).
our_argument_summary: All three c5 patches land cleanly. The moderate c5 hole (Scale comparison paragraph listing ellipse axis ratio as fourth parameter, creating internal self-contradiction) is fully resolved — both the V50.6 paragraph and Scale comparison paragraph now enumerate identical parameters (H₀ = 8,537, λ = 8,619, EW angular scale 0.9941, r_eq = 14,105). The finding and tldr_evidence now both say sub-millimetre, aligning with detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. One residual minor: tldr_verdict still says sub-meter instead of sub-millimetre, making it the sole remaining outlier across five text layers. Verdict Misleading holds with high confidence.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No change since c4/c5: no monitor.py domain, no find_curve.py regression. V50.6 H(r) parameters are static strings.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded throughout. 5.2% mean and 0.0% Oslo-Stockholm are static display.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Retreat-to-V50.6 remains closed (both stages evaluated). No new escape.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for WIN-027. Quadratic coefficients, R-squared, and V50.6 performance numbers are all static in dome site text.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The quadratic distance distortion is the azimuthal equidistant projection signature. Standard cartography predicts exactly this; the dome labels it a discovery.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Evolution note in raw-text/02-wins.txt: v22 circular-disc failure on southern/cross-equator distances patched by WIN-027 quadratic. Correction fitted AFTER the failure.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome geometry to quadratic coefficients. Values are regression outputs from unreproducible pipeline (find_curve.py is empty).

holes_found.description: tldr_verdict precision inconsistency: sole surviving sub-meter claim. Finding says sub-millimetre, tldr_evidence says sub-millimetre algorithmic precision, detail_evidence says sub-millimetre absolute accuracy, detail_verdict_text says sub-millimetre absolute accuracy. But tldr_verdict still reads: "Standard spherical geodesy achieves sub-meter precision globally with two parameters." This is a 1000x discrepancy (millimetre vs meter) in the one text layer the decider missed during the c5 precision-alignment patch. A dome defender can quote this specific sentence to argue our review is sloppy about its own benchmarks. The fix is a single word replacement.
holes_found.recommendation: In tldr_verdict, change "sub-meter precision" to "sub-millimetre precision" to match all other layers. This completes the precision alignment that c5 identified and the decider partially executed.

stronger_arguments.description: The Polaris-elevation parasitism argument is present in the V50.6 paragraph ("Polaris elevation equals latitude on a sphere") but buried in a subordinate clause within a technical sentence about coordinate conversion. This is the sharpest single sentence in the entire WIN-027 case — the dome's distance model literally cannot compute r(city) without knowing the latitude, which is only defined on a sphere. A standalone sentence or short paragraph explicitly calling this out as "parasitism on globe coordinates" would make it unmissable. Currently, a reader who skims the V50.6 paragraph may not register the implication.

The Oslo-Stockholm tautology: the text correctly notes both cities are at "nearly identical latitude" and the distance is "dominated by the Δθ scale factor 0.9941." But it does not draw the explicit conclusion that 0.0% error on a near-constant-latitude pair is a mathematical tautology when you have a fitted longitude scale factor — not a prediction success. The word "tautology" or "circular" would sharpen this from observation to indictment.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that projecting a round Earth onto a flat disc produces systematic, latitude-dependent distance distortions with approximately quadratic character. This is real mathematics.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The distortion IS the fingerprint of spherical curvature projected onto a plane. The quadratic correction exists because the Earth is round; claiming it as dome physics inverts the causation.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-027 has converged nicely over six review cycles. The c5 moderate hole (internal self-contradiction between V50.6 paragraph and Scale comparison paragraph on the fourth parameter) is cleanly resolved — both now enumerate H₀, λ, EW scale, and r_eq with values, eliminating the strongest defender attack surface. The precision alignment is 4/5 complete — only tldr_verdict retains the old sub-meter claim, which is a single word fix.

The argument structure is now tight: kernel-of-truth opening acknowledges the real math, supersession acknowledgment evaluates both the old quadratic and new H(r), the post-hoc evidence is documented with the dome's own evolution note, the V50.6 paragraph exposes the globe-coordinate parasitism, and the Scale comparison delivers the kill (sub-millimetre vs 5.2%, two params vs four). The Sydney-Santiago long-haul kill shot is the closer and it's devastating — 30,000 km disc chord vs 11,338 km globe distance is not a calibration error, it's a model failure.

Trajectory: c1 found the original issues, c2-c3 refined tags and citations, c4 found the ellipse-axis-ratio error and precision inconsistency, c5 verified partial fixes and found the surviving self-contradiction, c6 (this review) confirms the self-contradiction is resolved and only a minor tldr_verdict precision word remains. Confidence rises from 0.92 to 0.95. Next review should be a spot-check unless content changes again.

citations_verified: Vincenty 1975 DOI 10.1179/sre.1975.23.176.88 — correct (same DOI as c5)
Sydney-Santiago great-circle 11,338 km — verified c5 (haversine: 11,346 km)
r_eq = 14,105 km — matches raw-text/05-model.txt and WIN-057
H₀ = 8,537 km, λ = 8,619 km — matches raw-text/05-model.txt H(r) = 8537 x exp(-r/8619)
EW angular scale 0.9941 — matches raw-text/05-model.txt theta = delta_lon x 0.9941
WGS84 params (a=6,378,137 m, f=1/298.257223563) — standard values correct
citations_unchecked: QF27/QF28 operating schedule — still schedule-dependent but hedged in text

advocate_mode.best_defense: Post-patch, the internal self-contradiction attack is fully dead — the parameter list is now consistent throughout. The strongest remaining line for a dome defender is the single tldr_verdict sub-meter claim: "Your own summary says sub-meter but your detail says sub-millimetre. You cannot even state your own benchmark consistently." This is minor-league — it does not touch the verdict or any substantive argument — but it's the kind of gotcha that plays well in comment sections. After the one-word fix (sub-meter → sub-millimetre), the defender's attack surface reduces to: (1) contesting whether Vincenty/Karney sub-millimetre is a fair comparison to the dome's 5.2% (fair — the dome chose the benchmark by claiming distance accuracy), and (2) arguing the Sydney-Santiago route data is wrong (it is not — Qantas QF27/28 is well-documented). Neither touches the verdict.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Fix tldr_verdict sub-meter → sub-millimetre. After that fix, defense_survives drops to 0 for credibility attacks. The remaining substantive defenses (contesting benchmark fairness, disputing flight data) are weak and do not require text changes.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: The Scale comparison self-contradiction from c5 is resolved. WIN-027 and WIN-057 both cite r_eq = 14,105 km as the fourth parameter. WIN-058 cross-link (WGS-84 dependency through Polaris-elevation = latitude) is present in spirit (the V50.6 paragraph notes "Polaris elevation equals latitude on a sphere") but not as an explicit cross-reference. This is a stylistic gap, not a factual inconsistency.

claims_checked: H₀ = 8,537 km matches dome text — verified against raw-text/05-model.txt line 16
λ = 8,619 km matches dome text — verified against raw-text/05-model.txt line 16
r_eq = 14,105 km — consistent with WIN-057 and raw-text
WGS84 a=6,378,137 m and f=1/298.257223563 — standard geodetic values correct
quantitative_verification.computations: No new computations required — c5 verified all quantitative claims (Sydney-Santiago haversine, disc chord, adjusted R², chord/globe ratio). All remain accurate post-patch.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None. The "almost three times" ratio (actual 2.68x) remains hedged appropriately.
